name: create_task
description: Create a task in the user's Taskpile inbox. Returns the new task id and shape plus a `project` object ({id,name,shared,role,member_count}, or null) describing where it landed - use it to confirm a shared destination to the user, e.g. "added to the shared project 'indkøbsliste'". Tag syntax is `@tag_name`, NOT `#tag_name` - `#name` is the project shorthand and only the first `#` token in the title is used. Title may include one `#project_name` and any number of `@tag_name` tokens; both are stripped from the stored title. Project resolution for `#name`: if exactly one project you can reach (owned OR shared with you) has that name it's used; if several do (e.g. a private AND a shared one), the call FAILS with the candidate list - surface it and ask the user which, then retry with an explicit `projectId`; if none match, a new project YOU own is created (shorthand never writes into someone else's project). Pass `projectId` to target a specific project, including one shared with you - viewer access is rejected. To set tags at creation, use `@tag` in the title or pass `tags: string[]` (combined and de-duplicated). Optionally pass `notes` for markdown context, and `horizon` to schedule it in one call (skips placing it in inbox).

title.description: Task title. May include one `#project` and any number of `@tag` tokens. Use `@tag`, not `#tag`, for tags.

notes.description: Optional markdown notes / description for the task.

horizon.description: Optional time horizon. If set, the task skips the inbox.

projectId.description: Explicit project id (overrides #project shorthand). May be a project shared with you; viewer access is rejected. Look ids up via list_projects.

tags.description: Tags to attach at creation (creates new tags as needed). Equivalent to `@tag_name` shorthand in the title; both forms can be combined and are de-duplicated.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: list_tasks
description: List the user's OWN tasks (their personal buckets) - it does not pull in other members' tasks from shared projects; to list a shared project's tasks (all members) use search_tasks with that projectId. Status semantics: `inbox` = unsorted captures, `today` = scheduled for today, `later` = scheduled for a future date (tomorrow, in_n_days, next_week have an explicit horizon_target_date; someday has none), `delegated` = handed off (see follow_up_at), `done` = completed, `archived` = soft-deleted. With NO `status`/`statuses`, returns all OPEN tasks (inbox + today + later + delegated; done/archived excluded) - so 'my tasks' is never just the inbox. Pass `statuses: string[]` to combine specific ones, e.g. ['today','later']; or a single `status` to narrow.

status.description: Single status filter. Omit and use `statuses` for arrays.

statuses.description: Array of statuses. Use this instead of `status` to fetch multiple in one call.

project.description: Filter to a project by NAME (case-sensitive, same matching as the #shorthand) - e.g. 'taskpile' lists that project's tasks. Resolved server-side; errors if the name is unknown or matches more than one project. Mutually exclusive with projectId. Note: this still lists only YOUR tasks in that project - use search_tasks to see all members' tasks in a shared one.

projectId.description: Filter to a project by id. Pass null for tasks with no project. Mutually exclusive with `project`.

outputSchema.description: Tasks matching the filters, ordered by updated_at desc.

results.description: Tasks matching the filters, ordered by updated_at desc.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: get_task
description: Fetch a single task by id with full shape including notes and tags. Resolves the caller's own tasks and any task in a project shared with them (so a task surfaced by search can be opened).

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: update_task
description: Edit an existing task. Any field omitted is left unchanged. Pass `notes` (markdown) for the description body, or `notes: null` to clear. Pass `projectId: null` to clear the project, or a uuid to set/move it (the project must already exist - use list_projects; it may be one shared with you, and viewer access is rejected). When `projectId` is set the response carries a `project` object ({id,name,shared,role,member_count}) so you can confirm a shared destination to the user. `#project`/`@tag` shorthand in `title` is NOT parsed on update (unlike create_task) - to change tags use `tags`/`addTags`/`removeTags`, to change project use `projectId`. Tag fields are exclusive: pass `tags` to replace the entire tag list, or `addTags`/`removeTags` for delta updates, not both. Status lets you move a task between buckets without delegating or completing - to mark a task done use `complete_task`, to schedule use `assign_horizon`.

title.description: New title. Shorthand is NOT parsed here.

notes.description: Markdown notes/body. Pass null to clear.

projectId.description: Move to this project, or null to clear. The project must already exist.

status.description: Move the task to a different bucket. Prefer `complete_task` / `assign_horizon` / `delegate_task` for the common flows.

delegatedTo.description: Update delegation target without changing status. Pass null to clear. Use `delegate_task` for the full flow (status + follow-up in one call).

followUpAt.description: Update follow-up date (ISO date or datetime). Null clears it.

tags.description: Replace the entire tag list. Empty array clears all tags. Mutually exclusive with addTags/removeTags.

addTags.description: Tags to add (creates new tags as needed). Mutually exclusive with `tags`.

removeTags.description: Tags to remove. Mutually exclusive with `tags`.

notify.description: Toggle whether a push fires at the task's horizon time. Does not change the horizon itself - use `assign_horizon` to (re)schedule. Disabling never un-fires an already-sent notification, and enabling a task that already fired won't make it fire again.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: complete_task
description: Mark a task as done. If the task has a recurrence rule, the next instance is auto-created.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: uncomplete_task
description: Reverse a completion - restore a done task to the inbox so the user can re-schedule it. Use this when the agent (or the user) marked something complete by mistake. The task's horizon is cleared so the user actively re-decides.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: delete_task
description: Permanently delete a task. No archive, no undo. Prefer `archive_task` for reversible cleanup.

name: archive_task
description: Soft-delete a task by moving it to status='archived'. The task disappears from every active view but the row is retained so unarchive_task can bring it back. Prefer this over delete_task for routine cleanup.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: unarchive_task
description: Restore an archived task back to the inbox (no horizon, so the user re-decides where it belongs).

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: list_projects
description: List every project the user can reach - ones they own AND ones shared with them (project invitation or team) - with task counts. Each row carries `shared`, `role`, `member_count` and a `capabilities` object (e.g. `can_add_tasks`) so you can distinguish a private project from a shared one of the same name, pick the right `projectId` for a write, and know up front whether a write will be allowed. Active by default - pass includeArchived=true to also surface archived projects.

includeArchived.description: Include archived projects in the response. Default false.

outputSchema.description: Projects the user owns or is a member of, with task_count and sharing info.

results.description: Projects the user owns or is a member of, with task_count and sharing info.

team_id.description: Set when the project is shared.

task_count.description: Active task count in the project.

shared.description: True when the project is shared (team project or more than one member).

role.description: The caller's role in this project. Viewers cannot add or move tasks.

capabilities.description: Self-describing permissions derived from role, so you don't have to map role->permission yourself or attempt a write to discover it.

can_add_tasks.description: Whether the caller may add or move tasks into this project.

name: create_project
description: Create a project. Returns the new project id. Project names must be unique per user.

color.description: Optional hex color (e.g. '#3b82f6') for the project pill.

team_id.description: Set when the project is shared.

name: update_project
description: Rename a project and/or change its color. Only provided fields are updated. Setting archived flips the soft-delete flag (use archive_project / unarchive_project for clarity unless you're updating multiple fields at once).

color.description: Hex color for the project pill, or null to clear.

archived.description: Flip the soft-delete flag. Prefer archive_project / unarchive_project.

team_id.description: Set when the project is shared.

name: archive_project
description: Soft-delete a project. The project hides from list_projects (unless includeArchived=true) and from the project picker. Tasks inside it are untouched - they keep referring to the project, so unarchiving brings everything back.

team_id.description: Set when the project is shared.

name: unarchive_project
description: Restore an archived project to active.

team_id.description: Set when the project is shared.

name: list_tags
description: List the user's existing tags. Call this before inventing new @tag names in create_task - picking an existing tag keeps the user's tag-list clean.

outputSchema.description: All tags belonging to the user.

results.description: All tags belonging to the user.

name: rename_tag
description: Rename a tag for the user. All tasks tagged with the old name will now show the new name (tags are joined by id, not text). Names are unique per user - renaming to an existing name will fail.

newName.description: New tag name. Must not collide with an existing tag.

name: delete_tag
description: Permanently delete a tag and unlink it from every task it was attached to. The tasks themselves are untouched. No undo.

name: search_tasks
description: Search across every task the user can read: their own tasks PLUS tasks other members added to projects shared with them. `query` is a case-insensitive substring matched against title and (when includeBody=true) notes. Additional filters narrow the result set further; all are AND-combined. Filtering by `projectId` on a shared project returns every member's tasks in it. Omit `query` to filter purely by metadata. Archived tasks are excluded by default - pass includeArchived=true to include them. Note: the `tags` filter only matches the caller's own tags, so it won't surface other members' tasks by tag.

query.description: Substring to search for. Optional - if omitted you can still filter by tags / project / horizon / date range.

includeBody.description: Also search the task notes/body, not just the title. Default false.

tags.description: Filter to tasks that have ALL of these tag names.

project.description: Filter to a project by NAME (case-sensitive, same matching as the #shorthand) - e.g. 'taskpile'. Resolved server-side; errors if unknown or ambiguous. Mutually exclusive with projectId.

projectId.description: Filter to tasks in this project by id. Pass null for tasks without a project. Mutually exclusive with `project`.

horizons.description: Filter to tasks with one of these time_horizon values.

statuses.description: Filter by status. Default excludes 'archived' - overrides includeArchived if set.

dateFrom.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD - only tasks with horizon_target_date >= this date. Inclusive.

dateTo.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD - only tasks with horizon_target_date <= this date. Inclusive.

includeArchived.description: Include archived tasks. Ignored if `statuses` is set.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: assign_horizon
description: Set when a task should be done. Pass EITHER `horizon` (named bucket) OR `date` (explicit ISO date like '2026-05-20') - not both. Named buckets: `today_morning/afternoon/evening` → Today; `tomorrow`/`in_n_days`/`next_week`/`someday` → Later with the corresponding date. Explicit `date` resolves to today/in_n_days automatically (avoids LLM date arithmetic). Pass inNDays to override the user's default for the `in_n_days` bucket. Pass `atTime` (HH:MM) to fire at a specific wall-clock time instead of the bucket's slot hour. Pass `notify` to override whether a push fires at the horizon's wall-clock time; default follows the user's notify_on_horizon_default setting the first time a horizon is set on the task.

date.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) when the task should be done. Mutually exclusive with `horizon`. Past dates are rejected.

inNDays.description: Override the user's default for the `in_n_days` bucket.

atTime.description: Optional wall-clock time override in the user's timezone, formatted HH:MM (24h, e.g. '14:30'). Overrides the bucket's default slot hour, so the push fires at this exact time on the horizon's date. Omit to use the slot hour (morning/afternoon/evening or the morning hour for future buckets).

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: delegate_task
description: Mark a task as delegated to someone, with a follow-up date. The task disappears from Today until the follow-up date.

delegatedTo.description: Who the task is delegated to (free text - name, team, system).

followUpAt.description: When to follow up (ISO date or datetime). Defaults to 7 days from now if omitted.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: move_to_project
description: Bulk-assign a list of task ids to a project. Pass projectId: null to clear the project. The project must already exist (use list_projects) and may be one shared with you - viewer access is rejected. The response includes a `project` object ({id,name,shared,role,member_count}, or null) so you can confirm a shared destination to the user. Only the caller's own tasks are moved.

projectId.description: Destination project id (UUID), or null to clear the project on every task.

name: bulk_complete
description: Mark several tasks done in one round-trip. Recurrence is intentionally skipped here - use complete_task one at a time if you need recurring follow-ups.

name: bulk_delete
description: Permanently delete several tasks. No archive, no undo. Prefer letting the user delete via the UI for safety. Capped at 200 ids per call.

name: bulk_archive
description: Soft-delete several tasks in one round-trip by moving them to status='archived'. The recommended bulk cleanup - reversible (unarchive_task) and preferred over bulk_delete. Already-archived ids are skipped, so the count reflects real transitions. Capped at 200 ids per call.

name: bulk_assign_horizon
description: Set the same horizon on several tasks in one round-trip. Same horizon/date semantics as `assign_horizon` - pass either `horizon` or `date`, not both.

date.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD. Mutually exclusive with `horizon`.

inNDays.description: Override the user's default for the `in_n_days` bucket.

name: get_today
description: One-shot read of everything that should land on the user's Today screen: overdue tasks (a committed today task whose day has passed), today's committed tasks, scheduled tasks whose planned day has arrived (NOT auto-committed - they resurface for a review decision so a parked batch doesn't flood Today), delegated tasks with due follow-ups, and tasks completed today. Saves the three or four round-trips an agent would otherwise need to assemble this view. Returns { overdue, today, scheduled, followUp, completedToday }.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: get_review_queue
description: Return tasks waiting to be triaged - the review pile, everything whose review date has arrived: fresh inbox captures (no horizon), scheduled tasks whose parked day has landed, and yesterday's un-touched committed-today tasks (a horizon is a 'review me again on this date' marker, not a deadline). Mirrors the /inbox + /review screens. Future-parked, someday, delegated and done tasks are excluded. Note: a resurfaced task still carries its old status (later/today) until you act on it - membership here is derived from the date, not the stored status. Use this to drive a daily-review flow. Read-only tasks (viewer on a shared project) are excluded since no triage action would be allowed on them. Returns { inbox, count }.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: changes_since
description: Return tasks whose `updated_at` is strictly newer than the given ISO timestamp. Use this for incremental sync - pass the timestamp from the last response back next call. **Caveat:** deletions are NOT reported (no tombstone table); if the agent has a cached copy and a task disappears server-side, a periodic full reconcile is needed. Capped at 500 rows.

updatedSince.description: ISO datetime - return tasks updated strictly after this point.

statuses.description: Optional status filter. Omit to include every status.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: set_recurrence
description: Set or clear a task's repeat rule. Pass rule: null to make the task non-recurring. For weekly, `weekdays` is an array of ISO 8601 weekdays where 1=Monday and 7=Sunday. For monthly nth-weekday patterns ("last Friday", "second Wednesday"), combine `set_pos` (1..4 for nth, -1 for last) with a single-entry `weekdays`. End conditions: `until: YYYY-MM-DD` stops spawning past that date; `count: N` limits the total number of remaining occurrences (including the current task). When a recurring task is completed, the next instance is auto-created if neither end condition is exhausted.

rule.description: New recurrence rule, or null to clear repeat.

set_pos.description: Monthly nth-weekday: 1..4 for first..fourth, -1 for last. Combined with weekdays.

until.description: End condition: stop spawning past this calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

count.description: End condition: remaining occurrences including the current task. Each completion decrements this on the next instance; spawning stops when count reaches 1.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: get_morning_digest
description: Read the user's morning digest email preferences. Returns enabled, target email, mode (combined/per_project), hour (0-23), and IANA timezone. Use this before suggesting edits via configure_morning_digest.

name: configure_morning_digest
description: Configure the daily morning digest email. All fields are optional - only provided fields are updated. The digest summarises today's tasks, overdue items, delegated follow-ups, and new inbox captures, delivered at the chosen local hour. Pass `email: ""` to clear an override and fall back to the user's account email.

enabled.description: Turn the digest on or off. Off = no email sent regardless of other settings.

email.description: Override delivery address. Empty string clears the override and uses the account email.

mode.description: `combined` (default) = one email with everything. `per_project` = one email per project + an Inbox email for tasks without a project.

hour.description: Local hour (0-23) to send the digest. 7 = 07:00.

name: get_archived_retention
description: Read the user's auto-delete policy for archived tasks. Returns `days`: 0 = never auto-delete (the default); a positive N = archived tasks are permanently deleted N days after they were archived. Use this before changing it via configure_archived_retention.

name: configure_archived_retention
description: Set how long archived tasks are kept before they are permanently deleted. `days: 0` turns auto-delete off (never). A positive N permanently deletes any archived task whose archive date is older than N days, enforced by a daily job. Only archived tasks are affected - active and done tasks are never touched. This causes permanent data loss once a window elapses, so confirm the value with the user first.

days.description: Retention window in days. 0 = never auto-delete. Common choices: 30, 90, 365. Max 3650 (10 years).

name: create_team
description: Create a new team. The caller becomes the team owner. Teams own shared projects (see `convert_project_to_shared`) and the owner can invite editor/viewer members.

name: list_teams
description: List teams the user is a member of, with the user's role on each (owner/editor/viewer).

outputSchema.description: Teams the user is a member of.

results.description: Teams the user is a member of.

name: get_team
description: Fetch a team's full state: team row, member list with roles, pending invitations, and the caller's own role on the team.

items.description: A team_members row, enriched with profile fields.

name: update_team
description: Rename a team. Owner only.

name: delete_team
description: Permanently delete a team. Cascades to all shared projects, their tasks, members, and invitations. Owner only. No undo.

name: list_team_members
description: List members of a team with role, joined_at, display_name and email. Any member can call this.

items.description: A team_members row, enriched with profile fields.

name: update_member_role
description: Change a member's role between 'editor' and 'viewer'. Cannot promote/demote to/from 'owner' (use ownership transfer flow). Owner only.

role.description: New role. 'owner' cannot be set via this verb.

name: remove_team_member
description: Remove a member from a team. Owner can remove anyone except themselves. A non-owner member can pass their own userId to leave. Owner cannot leave - transfer or delete the team first.

userId.description: Member to remove. Pass your own userId to leave the team yourself.

name: invite_to_team
description: Invite someone to a team by email. Creates an invitation with a single-use token, expires in 14 days, and sends a transactional email to the recipient. Owner only. The invited email must accept via `accept_invitation` from a Taskpile account where the email matches. A team is capped at 10 active email invitations - if you hit the cap, ask the owner to revoke older invitations or wait for them to be accepted/expire. An optional `personalMessage` (max 500 chars) is included in the email body.

personalMessage.description: Optional personal note (max 500 chars) shown in the invitation email body.

name: list_invitations
description: List invitations on a team (pending + accepted history). Any member can read.

name: list_received_invitations
description: List pending invitations addressed to the authenticated user (both team and project invitations), so an agent can surface them and accept on the user's behalf. Each entry includes the project/team name, the inviter's email, the role, and an `id` to pass to accept_invitation. Excludes already-accepted and expired invitations. This is the only way to discover an invitation you RECEIVED - list_invitations is team-scoped and shows invitations a team admin SENT.

outputSchema.description: Pending invitations addressed to the caller.

results.description: Pending invitations addressed to the caller.

name: revoke_invitation
description: Delete a pending invitation. Owner only. Has no effect if the invitation has already been accepted.

name: accept_invitation
description: Accept a team OR project invitation. Pass either the single-use `token` from the email, or the `invitationId` from list_received_invitations / get_context (so you can accept on the user's behalf without them pasting a token). Validates expiry + that the caller's account email matches the invitation, then adds them as a member and marks it accepted. The response carries `scope` (team|project) and `name` (the team/project just joined) so you can confirm directly to the user without a follow-up list_projects / list_teams call.

token.description: Single-use invitation token from the email.

scope.description: Whether the invitation joined a team or a single project.

name.description: Name of the team/project just joined, so you can confirm it to the user without a follow-up lookup.

name: convert_project_to_shared
description: Convert a personal project into a shared project owned by a team. IRREVERSIBLE - shared projects cannot be converted back to personal. The team owner becomes the default triager. By default, existing tasks in the project are auto-assigned to the converter so they don't disappear from anyone's Today. Caller must own the project AND be owner/editor of the team.

teamId.description: Team id (UUID) that will own the project.

keepExistingTasksAssignedToMe.description: If true (default), sets assignee_id = caller on every existing task in the project so they keep showing up in the caller's Today/Later. Pass false to leave them unassigned (visible only in the project view).

team_id.description: Set when the project is shared.

name: set_project_triager
description: Set (or clear) the triager on a shared project. The triager is the team member who sees unassigned tasks from this project in their personal review queue. Pass userId: null to clear. Owner only. The triager must be a team member.

userId.description: Team member to make triager, or null to clear.

team_id.description: Set when the project is shared.

name: assign_task
description: Assign (or unassign) a task in a shared project to a team member. The assignee then sees the task in their Today/Later/Inbox based on the task's status. Pass assigneeId: null to unassign and return the task to the project's unassigned pile. Only valid for tasks in shared projects. Caller must be owner/editor of the project's team.

taskId.description: Task id (UUID) in a shared project.

assigneeId.description: Team member to assign to, or null to unassign.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: update_project_auto_assign
description: Set the per-project default-assignee strategy for a shared project. New tasks created in this project will be assigned according to `strategy`: 'creator' (default for new shared projects - the task creator gets the assignment), 'owner' (the team owner gets every new task), 'specific' (a fixed member identified by `userId` gets every new task), or 'unassigned' (tasks land in the triager's review pile). Caller must be owner or editor of the project's team. Personal projects ignore this setting.

strategy.description: Auto-assign strategy for newly created tasks.

userId.description: Required when strategy='specific'. Must be a team member.

team_id.description: Set when the project is shared.

name: move_task_to_private
description: Pull a task out of a shared project so it becomes a private inbox task on the caller's account. Allowed for the task's creator OR the project's owner. The task's project_id and assignee_id are cleared and status is reset to 'inbox'. Audit-logged so the team can see where it went.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: unshare_project
description: Pull a whole shared project back to a personal project. Strict - only allowed when the project contains no tasks from any other team member AND the team_audit_log has no foreign actor entries against this project. Caller must be the project's original owner. To free up a project that doesn't qualify, the owner must first move or delete the foreign tasks.

team_id.description: Set when the project is shared.

name: invite_to_project
description: Invite someone to a single project by email. Creates an invitation with a single-use token, expires in 14 days, and sends a transactional email to the recipient. Owner-of-project only. The invited email accepts via `accept_invitation`. Free-tier projects are capped at 4 people total (owner + 3 others, counting members + active invitations).

personalMessage.description: Optional personal note (max 500 chars) shown in the email body.

name: request_project_edit_access
description: Ask a shared project's owner for edit access. Caller must currently be a read-only viewer of the project. Notifies the owner by email (deduped to once an hour); the owner grants access by switching the caller's role to editor. Rate-limited per requester (a few per project per hour, and a modest global cap) - do not call in a loop; one request is enough.

projectId.description: Project id (UUID) you currently have viewer access to.

name: list_project_members
description: List people with access to a shared project, including their role (owner/editor/viewer), display_name, email, and is_me flag. Caller must be a member.

name: update_project_member_role
description: Change a project member's role between 'editor' and 'viewer'. Owner-of-project only. Owner role cannot be set via this verb.

role.description: New role. 'owner' cannot be set via this verb.

name: remove_project_member
description: Remove a member from a shared project. Owner can remove anyone except themselves; any non-owner member can remove themselves (leave). The departing member's tasks in this project are re-assigned to the project's triager (falling back to project owner).

userId.description: Member to remove. Non-owners may pass their own id to leave.

name: list_unassigned_tasks
description: List unassigned tasks in shared projects. If `projectId` is provided, returns unassigned tasks for that one project (any team member can call). Without `projectId`, returns unassigned tasks across every shared project where the caller is the triager - the data feed for the triager's review queue.

projectId.description: Optional: scope to one project. Omit to get the caller's full triager pile.

editable.description: Whether the caller may write this task (own it AND not a viewer of its project). False = read-only for you; skip write tools.

horizon_target_at.description: Absolute UTC instant the horizon resolves to (date + wall-clock time).

notify.description: Whether a push notification fires at horizon_target_at.

tags.description: Tag names attached to the task.

name: whoami
description: Identify the authenticated Taskpile account and return current state. Returns `user_id`, `email`, `display_name`, `account_created_at`, a `usage` block (task counts by status, active project count, tag count), a `teams` array (each team the user is a member of with role + joined_at) and a `subscription` block (tier, status, next_payment_at, per-tier limits). Use this to greet the user by name, surface their current consumption against limits, list their teams, or remind them of the next billing date. Billing is not yet live during beta - the subscription block will report tier='free' with null limits and a note until Paddle integration ships.

outputSchema.description: Identity + usage + subscription snapshot for the authenticated user.

name: get_context
description: One-shot orientation snapshot so you don't need list_projects + list_tags + list_tasks + an invitations probe on every turn. Returns: `projects` (everything the user can reach, each with shared/role/member_count), `tags` (names), `inbox` and `today` (true `count` + a compact list of up to 100 tasks each with id/title/status/project_name/time_horizon/horizon_target_date/horizon_target_at), and `received_invitations` (pending invitations addressed to the user, with count + id/scope/name/role/inviter - surface these and offer to accept via accept_invitation). `inbox` is the review pile (captures + scheduled tasks whose day has arrived + yesterday's un-touched committed-today), date-derived to match the /inbox screen - a resurfaced task still reads status later/today until acted on, so check `status`. `today` is only what was deliberately placed there today (slipped tasks move to the inbox pile, not here). inbox/today are the user's own tasks; use search_tasks to see a shared project's full task list.

outputSchema.description: Projects, tags, inbox/today snapshot, and pending invitations.

count.description: True total (the task list is capped at 100).

name: get_inbox_email
description: Return the user's per-account email-inbox alias (e.g. inbox-a3f7c2e9b4d18650@taskpile.app). Mails sent to this address create tasks in the inbox. Returns null if the user hasn't generated one yet - point them to Settings → Email to your inbox. NEVER call regenerate from an agent; that's a Settings-only action by design.

outputSchema.description: Email-inbox alias for the user, if minted.

name: search
description: Search the user's tasks. Returns ranked results with id, title, snippet, and a deep link. Optimized for ChatGPT Deep Research / Company Knowledge surfaces (OpenAI Apps SDK schema). For richer filtering use `search_tasks` instead.

query.description: Substring to search task titles for.

name: fetch
description: Fetch the full content of a single task by id. Returns id, title, text (markdown body), url, and metadata. OpenAI Apps SDK companion to `search`.
